name: Mustard Caviar
subtitle: Little probiotic pearls that pop
blrNote: Indian Yellow Mustard (Rai) is perfect. The black ones are more bitter and take longer to mellow.
tldr: Seeds + brine. They swell into pearls that lose their bitter bite and become tangy.

steps.title: Mix
steps.instruction: Put seeds in a jar. Cover with brine.

steps.title: Absorb
steps.instruction: Check after 12 hours. The seeds will have soaked up the water. Add more brine to keep them covered.

steps.title: Age
steps.instruction: Wait 10 days. The sharp "wasabi" bite will mellow into a sophisticated tang.

images.caption: Mustard Caviar
images.alt: Mustard Caviar — Little probiotic pearls that pop

culturalContext.story: A "chef secret" for adding texture to plates. It looks like expensive caviar but costs pennies.
culturalContext.significance: Unlike store-bought mustard, this is raw and probiotic.
culturalContext.funFact: If you blend this after fermenting, you get the best "Dijon" style mustard you've ever tasted.
